items.title: Nicotine exacerbates MASH via inducing intestinal dysbiosis and barrier dysfunction
items.author: Dazhi Chen
items.content: Nicotine exacerbates MASH by disrupting gut microbiota and impairing intestinal barrier integrity via HIF-1α suppression, while probiotic supplementation mitigates nicotine-induced liver injury

items.title: Rotational migration in human pancreatic ductal organoids depends on actin and myosin activity
items.author: Jerome Irianto
items.content: Pancreatic ductal organoids serve as a primary human model to study rotational migration — a process driven by myosin-dependent contractility, actin polymerization, and Arp2/3-mediated actin branching.

items.title: MicroRNA-mediated neuronal detargeting alters astrocyte cell fate conversion trajectories in vivo
items.author: Carol Schuurmans
items.content: In vivo astrocyte-to-neuron conversion is driven by Ascl1SA6 expression restricted to astrocytes via miR-124-target sites, with astrocyte pre-labeling marking reprogrammed neurons, confirmed by single-cell transcriptomics and pseudotime analysis.

items.title: Polymyxins slow down lateral diffusion of proteins and lipopolysaccharide in the E. coli outer membrane
items.author: Syma Khalid
items.content: Multi-scale molecular dynamics simulations reveal that polymyxins form polymyxin-protein aggregates upon associating with the E. coli outer membrane thereby reducing lateral mobility of outer membrane proteins and lipopolysaccharides.

items.title: A genome-wide association study identifies the GPM6A locus associated with age at onset in ALS
items.author: Gen Sobue
items.content: A genome-wide association study in Japanese patients with ALS identifies rs113161727 at the ADAM29-GPM6A locus as a modifier of age at onset and links it to GPM6A upregulation.

items.title: Tenascin-C from the tissue microenvironment promotes muscle stem cell maintenance and function through Annexin A2
items.author: Alessandra Sacco
items.content: Tenascin-C (TnC) produced by the fibro-adipogenic progenitors (FAPs) is required for MuSC maintenance and function. FAP-secreted TnC signals through Annexin-A2 on the MuSC surface to promote self-renewal and regeneration potential.

items.title: Neuroticism heterogeneity through item-level associations in resting-state functional connectivity
items.author: Martin P. Paulus
items.content: Brain functional connectivity analysis in a large cohort reveals neuroticism’s heterogeneous subcomponents, linked to genetic variation and psychological factors through distinct resting-state networks, suggesting an intermediate phenotype bridging genes and mind.

items.title: Structural mechanisms and insights on multiple nanobodies binding diverse SOD1 epitopes
items.author: Yu Ding
items.content: Structural basis for multi-nanobody recognition of SOD1 reveals distinct epitopes and enhanced avidity, guiding ALS therapeutics.
